subject: Investigate open-source SPSS alternatives for psychometric analysis

body.summary: We need a robust open-source alternative to SPSS for psychometric analysis (CFA, bifactor modeling, ICC, factor analysis). Current tooling (semopy in Python) produces Fisher Information Matrix issues and limited output. Please investigate: (1) GNU PSPP (SPSS clone), (2) JASP (R-based GUI, does CFA via lavaan), (3) jamovi (R-based GUI), (4) R + lavaan directly (gold standard for SEM/CFA). Evaluate: license compatibility (MIT/Apache/BSD only), CFA/bifactor support, reliability analysis (ICC, kappa), ease of integration with our Python pipeline, whether it runs on macOS arm64 and Linux amd64.
body.context: Running DA moderator Phase 1 — semopy CFA produces non-PD Fisher Information Matrix warnings and lacks standardized estimates. The bifactor model comparison needs more robust SEM tooling. R + lavaan provides the gold standard but requires R installation and rpy2 bridge.
requirements: Bifactor CFA with standardized loadings and proper standard errors
Model comparison (chi-square difference, AIC, BIC, RMSEA, CFI)
Runs on macOS arm64 (local) and Linux amd64 (chromabook)
License: MIT, Apache 2.0, or BSD only

claims.claim: semopy produces FIM non-PD warnings on our bifactor models
